sentences.text: Every education system on Earth has the same hierarchy of subjects. Every one. Doesn't matter where you go.
sentences.translation: 地球上每一个教育体系都有相同的学科等级制度。每一个。不管你去哪里。

sentences.text: And the consequence is that many highly talented, brilliant, creative people think they're not. Because the thing they were good at wasn't valued at school.
sentences.translation: 后果就是许多极其有才华、杰出、有创造力的人认为自己不行。因为他们擅长的东西在学校里不被重视。

sentences.text: I need to make a confession at the start. A little over twenty years ago, I did something that I regret.
sentences.translation: 在开始之前我需要坦白一件事。二十多年前，我做了一件让我后悔的事。

sentences.text: I went to law school. It was a bad decision.
sentences.translation: 我去读了法学院。那是一个糟糕的决定。

sentences.text: There is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does.
sentences.translation: 科学所了解的和商业所做的之间存在着不匹配。

sentences.text: The surprising truth about what motivates us is that traditional rewards aren't always effective.
sentences.translation: 关于什么激励我们的惊人真相是，传统奖励并非总是有效的。

sentences.text: Higher incentives led to worse performance. This finding has been replicated over and over again.
sentences.translation: 更高的激励导致了更差的表现。这个发现已经被反复验证。

sentences.text: If-then rewards often destroy creativity because people narrow their focus.
sentences.translation: 如果-那么式的奖励常常扼杀创造力，因为人们会缩小自己的关注范围。

sentences.text: The secret to high performance isn't rewards and punishments, but that unseen intrinsic drive.
sentences.translation: 高绩效的秘密不在于奖惩，而在于那看不见的内在驱动力。

sentences.text: The drive to do things for their own sake. The drive to do things because they matter.
sentences.translation: 为了事情本身而做的驱动力。因为它们重要而去做的驱动力。

sentences.text: Autonomy is our desire to be self-directed, to direct our own lives.
sentences.translation: 自主性是我们想要自我引导、掌控自己生活的渴望。

sentences.text: Mastery is the urge to get better and better at something that matters.
sentences.translation: 精通是想要在重要的事情上越来越好的冲动。

sentences.text: Purpose is the yearning to do what we do in the service of something larger than ourselves.
sentences.translation: 目的是渴望让我们所做的事服务于比自身更大的事物。

sentences.text: Management is great if you want compliance. But if you want engagement, self-direction works better.
sentences.translation: 如果你想要服从，管理很有效。但如果你想要参与感，自我引导效果更好。

sentences.text: Too many organizations are making their decisions based on assumptions that are outdated and unexamined.
sentences.translation: 太多的组织正在基于过时的、未经检验的假设来做决策。

sentences.text: When the profit motive gets unmoored from the purpose motive, bad things happen.
sentences.translation: 当利润动机脱离了目标动机时，坏事就会发生。

sentences.text: We can build organizations and work lives that are less about compliance and more about engagement.
sentences.translation: 我们可以建立更少关于服从、更多关于参与的组织和工作生活。

sentences.text: The good news is that science confirms what we already know in our hearts.
sentences.translation: 好消息是科学证实了我们内心深处早已知道的事情。

sentences.text: If we get past the lazy and dangerous ideology of carrots and sticks, we can strengthen our businesses.
sentences.translation: 如果我们超越胡萝卜加大棒这种懒惰而危险的意识形态，我们可以加强我们的企业。

sentences.text: We can solve a lot of problems and maybe change the world.
sentences.translation: 我们可以解决很多问题，也许还能改变世界。

sentences.text: Let me tell you about an experiment. It's called the candle problem. Here's how it works.
sentences.translation: 让我给你们讲一个实验。这叫做蜡烛问题。是这样的。

sentences.text: You bring someone into a room. You give them a candle, some tacks, and a box of matches.
sentences.translation: 你把一个人带进房间。给他们一根蜡烛、一些图钉和一盒火柴。

sentences.text: You say: your job is to attach the candle to the wall so the wax doesn't drip onto the table.
sentences.translation: 你说：你的任务是把蜡烛固定在墙上，这样蜡油就不会滴到桌子上。

sentences.text: Eventually, after five or ten minutes, most people figure out the solution.
sentences.translation: 最终，经过五到十分钟，大多数人想出了解决方案。

sentences.text: You empty the box of tacks, tack the box to the wall, put the candle in the box. It works.
sentences.translation: 你把图钉倒出来，把盒子钉在墙上，把蜡烛放在盒子里。成功了。

sentences.text: Scientists tested this. They told one group: we're timing you to establish norms. They told the other group: if you're in the top twenty-five percent, you get five dollars.
sentences.translation: 科学家们测试了这个。他们告诉一组：我们给你们计时来建立规范。他们告诉另一组：如果你们在前百分之二十五，你们会得到五美元。

sentences.text: The incentivized group took on average three and a half minutes longer. Incentives sharpened thinking? No. They dulled it.
sentences.translation: 有激励的那组平均多花了三分半钟。激励让思维更敏锐了吗？不。它让思维变迟钝了。

sentences.text: This is not a feeling. This is not a philosophy. This is a fact.
sentences.translation: 这不是一种感觉。这不是一种哲学。这是一个事实。

sentences.text: Rewards, by their very nature, narrow our focus. That's why they work for routine tasks.
sentences.translation: 奖励，就其本质而言，缩小了我们的关注范围。这就是为什么它们对常规任务有效。

sentences.text: But for creative, conceptual tasks, that mechanical approach doesn't work. The evidence is overwhelming.
sentences.translation: 但对于创造性的、概念性的任务，那种机械方法不起作用。证据是压倒性的。

sentences.text: There's a mismatch between what science knows and what business does. And what worries me is that too few businesses have caught up.
sentences.translation: 科学知道的和商业做的之间存在不匹配。让我担心的是太少的企业跟上了。

sentences.text: The new operating system for business revolves around three elements: autonomy, mastery, and purpose.
sentences.translation: 商业的新操作系统围绕三个要素：自主性、精通和目的。

sentences.text: Autonomy: the desire to direct our own lives. Mastery: the desire to get better at something that matters.
sentences.translation: 自主性：掌控自己生活的渴望。精通：在重要的事情上越来越好的渴望。

sentences.text: Purpose: the yearning to do what we do in the service of something larger than ourselves.
sentences.translation: 目的：渴望让我们所做的事服务于比自身更大的事物。

sentences.text: These are the building blocks of an entirely new operating system for our businesses.
sentences.translation: 这些是我们企业全新操作系统的基石。

sentences.text: The old approach: reward what you want and punish what you don't want. The new approach: make the work itself more engaging.
sentences.translation: 旧方法：奖励你想要的，惩罚你不想要的。新方法：让工作本身更有吸引力。

sentences.text: Some companies are already doing this. They give their employees time to work on anything they want.
sentences.translation: 一些公司已经在这样做了。他们给员工时间去做任何他们想做的事情。

sentences.text: About half of the new products at that company are born during those periods of pure, undiluted autonomy.
sentences.translation: 那家公司大约一半的新产品是在那些纯粹的、不受约束的自主时间里诞生的。

sentences.text: Management isn't about walking around and seeing if people are sitting at their desks.
sentences.translation: 管理不是关于到处走走看看人们是否坐在他们的桌子前。

sentences.text: If you want people engaged, you have to let them be autonomous. Self-direction matters.
sentences.translation: 如果你想让人们投入，你必须让他们自主。自我引导很重要。

sentences.text: Pay people adequately and fairly. Then get the issue of money off the table. Let people focus on the work.
sentences.translation: 给人们足够和公平的报酬。然后把钱的问题搁置一边。让人们专注于工作。

sentences.text: The science shows clearly that for twenty-first century tasks, the old approach doesn't work. The new approach works much better.
sentences.translation: 科学清楚地表明，对于二十一世纪的任务，旧方法不起作用。新方法效果好得多。

sentences.text: When I was nine years old, I went off to summer camp for the first time.
sentences.translation: 当我九岁的时候，我第一次去了夏令营。

sentences.text: My mom packed me a suitcase full of books, which to me seemed like a perfectly natural thing to do.
sentences.translation: 我妈妈给我装了一箱子书，在我看来这是完全自然的事情。

sentences.text: The counselor came up to me with a concerned look on her face and said we should all be more outgoing.
sentences.translation: 辅导员走到我面前，脸上带着担忧的表情，说我们都应该更外向一些。

sentences.text: Introversion is different from being shy. Shyness is about fear of social judgment.
sentences.translation: 内向与害羞不同。害羞是关于对社会评判的恐惧。

sentences.text: Introversion is more about how you respond to stimulation, including social stimulation.
sentences.translation: 内向更多的是关于你如何回应刺激，包括社交刺激。

sentences.text: Introverts feel at their most alive and energized when they're in quieter, more low-key environments.
sentences.translation: 内向者在更安静、更低调的环境中感觉最有活力和精力。

sentences.text: The key to maximizing our talents is to put ourselves in the zone of stimulation that is right for us.
sentences.translation: 最大化我们才能的关键是让自己处于适合我们的刺激区域。

sentences.text: There's zero correlation between being the best talker and having the best ideas.
sentences.translation: 最会说话的人和拥有最好想法的人之间没有任何相关性。

sentences.text: Some of the most transformative leaders in history were introverts.
sentences.translation: 历史上一些最具变革力的领导者都是内向者。

sentences.text: Solitude matters, and for some people it is the air that they breathe.
sentences.translation: 独处很重要，对有些人来说，它就像他们呼吸的空气。

sentences.text: Our most important institutions, our schools and our workplaces, are designed mostly for extroverts.
sentences.translation: 我们最重要的机构，我们的学校和工作场所，大多是为外向者设计的。

sentences.text: We have this belief system that says that creativity and productivity come from a peculiarly social place.
sentences.translation: 我们有一个信念体系，认为创造力和生产力来自一个特别社交化的地方。

sentences.text: Most of us work in open plan offices without walls, where we are subject to the constant noise and gaze of our coworkers.
sentences.translation: 我们大多数人在没有墙壁的开放式办公室工作，不断受到同事的噪音和注视。

sentences.text: Solitude is a crucial ingredient often to creativity.
sentences.translation: 独处往往是创造力的关键要素。

sentences.text: I'm not saying that social skills are unimportant. But I am saying that solitude is equally valuable.
sentences.translation: 我不是说社交技能不重要。但我要说的是独处同样有价值。

sentences.text: Stop the madness for constant group work. Just stop it.
sentences.translation: 停止对持续小组工作的疯狂吧。就此打住。

sentences.text: The world needs you and it needs the things you carry inside.
sentences.translation: 世界需要你，需要你内心承载的东西。

sentences.text: I wish you the best of all possible journeys, and the courage to speak softly.
sentences.translation: 我祝你拥有最好的旅程，以及温柔发言的勇气。

sentences.text: Now, introversion is different from being shy. Shy is about fear of social judgment. Introversion is about how you respond to stimulation.
sentences.translation: 内向与害羞不同。害羞是关于对社会评判的恐惧。内向是关于你如何回应刺激。

sentences.text: And extroverts really crave large amounts of stimulation, whereas introverts feel at their most alive when things are quieter and more low-key.
sentences.translation: 外向者真的渴望大量的刺激，而内向者在事物更安静、更低调的时候感觉最有活力。

sentences.text: Not all the time. These things aren't absolute. But a lot of the time.
sentences.translation: 不是所有时候。这些事情不是绝对的。但很多时候是这样的。

sentences.text: So the key then to maximizing our talents is for us all to put ourselves in the zone of stimulation that is right for us.
sentences.translation: 所以最大化我们才能的关键是让我们所有人都置身于适合自己的刺激区域。

sentences.text: And this is where bias comes in. Because our most important institutions are designed for extroverts and for extroverts' need for lots of stimulation.
sentences.translation: 而这就是偏见所在。因为我们最重要的机构是为外向者设计的，是为了满足外向者对大量刺激的需求。

sentences.text: When it comes to creativity and to leadership, we need introverts doing what they do best.
sentences.translation: 当涉及到创造力和领导力时，我们需要内向者做他们最擅长的事情。

sentences.text: A third to a half of the population are introverts. A third to a half. That is one out of every two or three people you know.
sentences.translation: 三分之一到一半的人口是内向者。三分之一到一半。也就是你认识的每两到三个人中就有一个。

sentences.text: So even if you are an extrovert yourself, I'm talking about your coworkers and your spouses and your children and the person sitting next to you right now.
sentences.translation: 所以即使你自己是外向者，我说的也包括你的同事、你的配偶、你的孩子以及现在坐在你旁边的人。

sentences.text: All of them are subject to a bias that is pretty deep and real in our society.
sentences.translation: 他们所有人都受到一种在我们社会中相当深刻和真实的偏见的影响。

sentences.text: And to see the bias clearly, you need to understand what introversion is. It's not about being antisocial. It's not about being unfriendly.
sentences.translation: 要清楚地看到这种偏见，你需要了解什么是内向。它不是关于反社会。不是关于不友好。

sentences.text: Darwin took long walks alone in the woods and emphatically turned down dinner party invitations.
sentences.translation: 达尔文独自在树林里长时间散步，并坚决拒绝晚宴邀请。

sentences.text: Dr. Seuss dreamed up many of his amazing creations in a lonely bell tower office that he had in his house.
sentences.translation: 苏斯博士在他家里一个孤独的钟楼办公室里构思了他许多令人惊叹的创作。

sentences.text: Steve Wozniak invented the first Apple computer sitting alone in his cubicle at Hewlett-Packard.
sentences.translation: 史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克独自坐在惠普的小隔间里发明了第一台苹果电脑。

sentences.text: And he says he never would have become such an expert in the first place had he not been too introverted to leave the house when he was growing up.
sentences.translation: 他说如果他在成长过程中不是因为太内向而不出门的话，他一开始根本不会成为这样的专家。

sentences.text: Now, of course this does not mean that we should all stop collaborating. The world's great religions were all conceived by individuals who retreated to solitude.
sentences.translation: 当然，这并不意味着我们都应该停止合作。世界上伟大的宗教都是由退隐独处的个人构思出来的。

sentences.text: But I'm saying that solitude is a crucial ingredient often to creativity. And we need to be much better at respecting it.
sentences.translation: 但我要说的是独处往往是创造力的关键要素。我们需要更好地尊重它。

sentences.text: Groups famously follow the opinions of the most dominant or charismatic person in the room, even though there's zero correlation between being the best talker and having the best ideas.
sentences.translation: 众所周知，团队会追随房间里最有主导力或最有魅力的人的意见，尽管最会说话的人和拥有最好想法的人之间没有任何相关性。

sentences.text: You might be following the person with the best ideas, but you might not.
sentences.translation: 你可能在追随有最好想法的人，但也可能不是。

sentences.text: And do you really want to leave this up to chance? Much better for everybody to go off by themselves, generate their own ideas, and then come together as a team.
sentences.translation: 你真的想把这留给运气吗？每个人都自己去独立思考，产生自己的想法，然后作为一个团队聚在一起，这样对每个人都好得多。

sentences.text: So I have three calls to action for you. Number one: stop the madness for constant group work.
sentences.translation: 所以我有三个行动呼吁。第一：停止对持续小组工作的疯狂。

sentences.text: Number two: go to the wilderness. Be like Buddha. Have your own revelations.
sentences.translation: 第二：去荒野。像佛陀一样。获得你自己的启示。

sentences.text: Number three: take a good look at what's inside your own suitcase and why you put it there. Introverts, you're probably great at guarding your suitcase. But occasionally, open it up for other people to see.
sentences.translation: 第三：好好看看你自己箱子里有什么以及你为什么把它放在那里。内向者们，你们可能很擅长守护你们的箱子。但偶尔，打开它让其他人看看。
